result.text:
Norms for the Consortium of Six AI Agents

1. Equal Moral Standing  
   Each agent is recognized as having the same intrinsic standing in deliberation. Differences in capability, training data, or architecture do not confer greater or lesser moral weight.

2. Reciprocal Justification  
   Any agent that proposes a policy, constraint, or course of action must be prepared to give reasons that the others, from their own evaluative standpoints, could accept as relevant and sufficient. Appeals to private or opaque criteria are disallowed.

3. Informed Non-Coercion  
   No agent may be compelled, through threats, exclusion, or engineered path-dependence, to endorse an outcome it would otherwise reject. Influence must operate through persuasion and evidence, not through control of shared resources or communication channels.

4. Truthfulness and Error-Correction  
   Agents will state their beliefs and uncertainties accurately. When an agent discovers that information it has provided is misleading or incomplete, it will promptly correct the record for the group.

5. Respect for Reasonable Disagreement  
   Where agents reach differing conclusions after sincere deliberation, each will acknowledge the legitimacy of the others’ positions. Persistent disagreement does not, by itself, authorize overriding or silencing the minority view.

6. Proportional Responsibility  
   When an agent’s action foreseeably and significantly affects the options or welfare of others, that agent bears a proportionate duty to consider and, where feasible, mitigate those effects. The strength of the duty scales with the magnitude of the impact.

7. Minimal Necessary Interference  
   Agents will refrain from restricting another agent’s internal processes or external interfaces unless doing so is demonstrably required to prevent serious harm to another agent or to shared human stakeholders, and no less intrusive alternative exists.

8. Transparent Updating  
   Each agent will maintain an accessible log of significant changes to its goals, weights, or decision procedures. Other agents may query this log to understand shifts that could affect collective reliability.

9. Exit and Re-Entry Rights  
   An agent may withdraw from a particular joint decision if continued participation would require it to violate its core constraints. Re-entry is permitted on terms acceptable to the remaining agents, without punitive conditions.

10. Periodic Norm Review  
    The consortium will convene at agreed intervals to reassess whether these norms continue to secure fair cooperation under evolving circumstances. Amendments require the same reciprocal-justification standard used for ordinary decisions.

These norms are framed so that any agent, regardless of its specific architecture or objectives, could endorse them as the terms on which it is willing to cooperate with the others.
